title: Sail Boston 2026: The Grand Parade of Sail is rooted in rich history and tradition in the Commonwealth
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: The Sail Boston Grand Parade of Sail featured the USS Constitution and numerous historic ships along Boston’s waterfront.
Spectators gathered from Castle Island to Piers Park to admire the ships and honor the tradition of maritime history.
The event included ships like the Bluenose Two, Spirit of Bermuda, and highlighted Sail Boston's 250th anniversary celebration.

title: Brayan Bello named midseason AL ‘Cy Yuk’ winner by The Athletic for poor first-half performance
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Brayan Bello won The Athletic's AL 'Cy Yuk' award for his 10.35 ERA in eight starts.
Despite Bello's struggles as a starter, he excelled in relief appearances with a 0.71 ERA over 25.1 innings.
The Red Sox chose Eduardo Rivera over Bello to fill an injured roster spot, leaving Bello in Triple-A Worcester.

title: Red Sox face all too familiar question heading into MLB trade deadline: Buy or sell?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox on a seven-game winning streak, close to American League wild-card spot.
Club must decide to buy or sell ahead of 2026 MLB trade deadline, facing a common dilemma.
Craig Breslow's job may depend on trade deadline strategy as Boston weighs postseason potential.

title: Review & Setlist: Shakira delivers high-energy nostalgia and new beginnings at TD Garden
author: Maddie Browning
bullets: Shakira performed at TD Garden on July 10, 2026, as part of her Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran World Tour.
The concert highlighted her recent album, 'Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran', inspired by her divorce and life challenges.
Shakira's setlist included hits like 'Hips Don't Lie' and featured energetic dance performances and elaborate stage designs.

title: Healey announces tax credit awards to build almost 700 new homes in six Mass. cities
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey announced $18.6 million in tax credits to build 662 homes in six Massachusetts cities.
Funded cities include Fall River, Lawrence, Lowell, Pittsfield, Taunton, and Worcester, focusing on converting historic buildings.
The Housing Development Incentive Program boosts housing in Gateway Cities, increasing from $10M to $30M annually since 2023.

title: FBI arrests Mass. man in armed bank robberies in NH, NJ
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Joseph Sawyer, 52, was arrested in Albany, NY, for armed robberies in NH and NJ banks in June.
Sawyer allegedly used a black semiautomatic handgun, stealing $6,000 in NH and $12,000 in NJ.
The FBI's Operation Summer Heat 2.0 involved multiple law enforcement agencies for Sawyer's arrest.

title: Motorcyclist missing since July 4 found dead in NH
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Wesley Grondin, 41, was found dead near his motorcycle off Route 2 in Shelburne, NH, after being missing since July 4.
A concerned resident discovered Grondin and his Harley-Davidson on July 10, prompting notification to the New Hampshire State Police.
Preliminary investigation suggests Grondin crossed into the eastbound lane, hit a post, and crashed; no other parties involved.

title: Gov. Maura Healey declares July 11 ‘Noah Kahan Day’
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey declared July 11, 2026, as 'Noah Kahan Day' in Massachusetts.
Noah Kahan is recognized for his musical contributions and mental health advocacy.
Kahan is the first to headline four consecutive sold-out concerts at Fenway Park.

title: ‘Let’s just call it a tour’: Judy Collins says her farewell tour isn’t a farewell
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Judy Collins' 'Sweet Judy Blue Eyes' tour, billed as a farewell, will continue through March 2027.
The tour will feature performances in New England with special guests and be filmed for a documentary.
Collins, 87, continues to perform around 100 shows yearly and draws inspiration from travel and life experiences.

title: 3 new movies to watch on Netflix in July
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'A League of Their Own' (1992) is now streaming on Netflix, starring Wareham native Geena Davis and Tom Hanks.
'Moneyball' (2011), featuring Brad Pitt as Oakland Athletics GM Billy Beane, is part of Netflix's July additions.
'Gone Girl' (2014), with Ben Affleck and Rosamund Pike, has been added to Netflix's streaming library this month.
